title: Field Technician
company: Pestalto Environmental Health Services Inc - Divisional Office
job_responsibilities: Candidates must be self-motivated, self-starters, able to work independently, and able to commit to working the full term from May 27th to the end of August. Employment in Ontario will begin on ca. May 27th and will continue through to the end of August (Labour Day). Employees must commit to working the full term of the contract to the end of August. Employees will be required to work 40 hours/week; 8 hours a day, Monday to Friday; 7 a.m. to 3:30 p.m. The work will involve the application of biological larvicides to the urban catch basins using motorized scooters and vehicles. Accepted candidates could face physically and mentally demanding outdoor work conditions and therefore must be highly motivated, energetic, committed, and in good physical shape. Outdoor work is a very considerable component of these positions, and sometimes the weather can be inclement during summer months.
required_skills: Candidates should have a valid G driver's license and provide evidence of driving experience and have a good driving record. Operation of company vehicles is expected. Summer employees will also need to be in possession of a valid pesticide technician's certificate (training, materials & testing provided). They must provide documentation that they have completed the on-line course for scooter operation (provided by the company). Candidates must agree to a 2-day orientation session in May and will be paid for their participation. This training will include all aspects of the job including product application, safe operation of a motor vehicle, and an in person rider training course offered by the Rider Training Institute (RTI) The applicator certification exam will be written prior to the orientation session and at no cost to the individual. It is considered an asset to be familiar with GPS technology and familiar with working from maps. Experience with safe scooter (or motorcycle) operation will also be considered an asset but not essential While there are no guarantees, some outstanding individuals who successfully completed a work term with Pestalto went on to work for Regional Health Units.

title: Accounting Assistant
company: University of Waterloo - Renison University College
job_responsibilities: Nature and Scope: The Accounting Assistant co-op role will assist the Finance staff with weekly accounting entries - primarily Accounts Payable, Account Reconciliations, Student Billing and other duties as assigned. The Finance office consists of a Director of Finance and Accounting Manager - this role will be closely working with both these roles in the department. Interpersonal Contacts: This position requires the incumbent to communicate with a wide range of people in various positions including department staff, directors, managers and faculty. Level of Responsibility: The candidate is expected to maintain ownership and responsibility of a queue of requests that range in scope including Account Payable entry, Student Billing reconciliation, Month end journal entries, and special projects directly related to the Finance office. The role has defined and standard tasks and receives guidance from the Accounting Manager. Decision Making: The Accounting Assistant needs to be able to identify problems and suggest solutions to the best of their ability. Guidance from both the Accounting Manager and Director of Finance will always be readily available. Summary of Key Accountabilities: Accounts Payable - Entry, approval review and final preparation for EFT/cheque runs, as well as sending out cheques and EFT remittances Journal Entry preparations, particularly month end entries Assisting with the year-end audit preparation for the external auditors- audit occurs in July Responsible for maintaining and ordering office supplies as needed by the College General Ledger account reconciliations Responsible for entering payments and maintaining the student billing tracking and system maintenance for non-credit programming
required_skills: Accounting knowledge would be a strong asset Detail oriented, High level of accuracy Proficient in Microsoft Office, particularly in Excel Strong organizational skills with the ability to work with multiple priorities and deadlines Superior communication skills, both oral and written Excellent problem-solving skills Proven ability to work independently and as part of a team Professional, tactful and diplomatic, with ability to maintain strict confidentiality
